message:
fix(macros): macro_start_session reuses pre-registered pane identity

Closes mcp_agent_mail#110.

Orchestrators like NTM pre-register an agent for each tmux pane at
boot time and write the canonical pane-identity file before launching
the agent process. When the agent then calls `macro_start_session`
without an explicit `agent_name`, register_agent was minting a fresh
random name (because the macro had no awareness of the pre-existing
identity), and the project ended up with two identities pointing at
the same logical pane:

- the orchestrator-registered name (used by file reservations and
  inbox writes from the orchestrator side)
- the macro-minted name (used by the agent's own outbound mail)

Both halves of the system would think they were "the agent" for that
pane and inbox routing would split between the two records.

`macro_start_session` now resolves the pane identity for the current
process before calling `register_agent`:

```
let resolved_name = agent_name.or_else(|| {
    pane_identity::resolve_identity_current_pane(&project.human_key)
        .and_then(|name| {
            models::normalize_agent_name(&name).or(Some(name))
        })
});
```

The fallback chain is:

1. Caller-supplied `agent_name` always wins (unchanged contract).
2. Otherwise, look up the pane identity for this process's tmux
   pane scoped to the project's `human_key`.
3. Normalize the resolved name through
   `models::normalize_agent_name` so casing / suffix conventions
   match the rest of the system; if normalization rejects the name
   (e.g. invalid chars), fall back to the raw resolved name rather
   than minting a new one — at least the names will collide
   deterministically.
4. Only if no pane identity exists does `register_agent` mint a
   fresh random name (the previous behavior).

Net effect: `macro_start_session` is now idempotent against the
orchestrator boot path. Repeated calls from the same pane reuse the
same identity instead of accumulating duplicate records.

Cargo.lock side: consolidated `windows-sys` to a single 0.61.2 entry
across the dependency tree (was a mix of 0.52.0, 0.59.0, 0.60.2,
0.61.2 from transitively pinned deps). No behavior change — just
fewer build-time variants of the Windows binding crate to compile.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -124,12 +124,28 @@ pub async fn macro_start_session(
     let project_json = crate::identity::ensure_project(ctx, human_key.clone(), None).await?;
     let project: ProjectResponse = parse_json(project_json, "project")?;
 
+    // Dedup against pre-existing pane identities (mcp_agent_mail#110).
+    //
+    // Orchestrators like NTM pre-register an agent for each tmux pane and
+    // write the canonical pane-identity file before booting the agent. When
+    // the agent then calls `macro_start_session` without an explicit
+    // `agent_name`, register_agent would mint a fresh random name and we'd
+    // end up with two identities pointing at the same logical pane. Look up
+    // the pane identity first and reuse the pre-registered name when one is
+    // available, so the macro is idempotent against the boot path.
+    let resolved_name = agent_name.or_else(|| {
+        mcp_agent_mail_core::pane_identity::resolve_identity_current_pane(&project.human_key)
+            .and_then(|name| {
+                mcp_agent_mail_core::models::normalize_agent_name(&name).or(Some(name))
+            })
+    });
+
     let agent_json = crate::identity::register_agent(
         ctx,
         project.human_key.clone(),
         program,
         model,
-        agent_name,
+        resolved_name,
         task_description,
         None,
         reaper_exempt,
